Title: Validating Legacy GPUs for Local LLM Inference

Introduction:
Legacy graphics processing units (GPUs) are an essential component of modern AI systems, providing the necessary computing power to perform complex tasks such as image and speech recognition. However, legacy GPUs may not be capable of handling the high computational demands associated with local language modeling (LLM). This note aims to provide guidance on validating legacy GPUs for local LLM inference by discussing the key considerations, best practices, and recommended tools.
